Test Name: Nightly schedule fires at configured cron
Description: Verifies the scheduler triggers the sync flow at the configured nightly time
Pre-conditions: App deployed and running; sync.cron set to a near-future time for testing
Test Steps: 1. Set sync.cron to fire in 1 minute | 2. Start the app | 3. Wait for the trigger time | 4. Inspect logs
Expected Result: The landowner-sync-scheduler-flow starts once and logs START landowner-sync with a correlationId

Description: Verifies a full nightly run completes purge fetch enrich build and upsert in order
Pre-conditions: Rainbow returns current registrations with owners; Salesforce reachable
Test Steps: 1. Trigger the scheduler flow | 2. Allow the run to complete | 3. Review logs and Salesforce records
Expected Result: Run completes with END landowner-sync upserted=N deleted=M skipped=S and LandOwner__c records present in Salesforce

Test Name: Delete records whose ToDate has passed
Description: Verifies expired records with no lawyer are deleted at run start
Pre-conditions: Salesforce seeded with a LandOwner__c where ToDate__c is in the past and Lawyer__c is null
Test Steps: 1. Seed an expired record | 2. Trigger run | 3. Query Salesforce for the record
Expected Result: The expired record is deleted and deleteCount is incremented

Description: Verifies records with a null ToDate are never deleted
Pre-conditions: Salesforce seeded with a LandOwner__c where ToDate__c is null
Test Steps: 1. Seed record with null ToDate__c | 2. Trigger run | 3. Query Salesforce for the record
Expected Result: The record remains in Salesforce and is not deleted

Description: Verifies expired records with a manually filled Lawyer are never deleted
Pre-conditions: Salesforce seeded with an expired LandOwner__c where Lawyer__c is populated
Test Steps: 1. Seed expired record with Lawyer__c set | 2. Trigger run | 3. Query Salesforce
Expected Result: The record is preserved because Lawyer__c is not null

Description: Verifies the purge step is skipped cleanly when nothing matches
Pre-conditions: Salesforce contains no records matching the stale criteria
Test Steps: 1. Ensure no stale records exist | 2. Trigger run | 3. Inspect logs
Expected Result: The choice guard skips delete deleteCount=0 and no error is raised

Description: Verifies the filter retrieves only isCurrentRegistration eq true rows
Pre-conditions: Rainbow Analytics OData reachable with mixed current and non-current rows
Test Steps: 1. Trigger fetch-registrations-subflow | 2. Capture outbound request | 3. Inspect returned set
Expected Result: Rainbow Analytics receives GET /registrations with the current filter and only current registrations are returned

Description: Verifies all pages are collected when results span multiple OData pages
Pre-conditions: Rainbow returns a response containing @odata.nextLink
Test Steps: 1. Configure Rainbow to return 2 pages | 2. Trigger fetch | 3. Count collected registrations
Expected Result: All 140 registrations are collected by following @odata.nextLink until exhausted

Description: Verifies each registration is projected to the expected internal shape
Pre-conditions: Rainbow returns one registration with all fields
Test Steps: 1. Trigger fetch | 2. Inspect vars.registrations
Expected Result: Each registration maps to registrationKey buildingCode propertyKey projectCode righttype and plot

Test Name: Fetch active owners for a registration
Description: Verifies owners with null toDate are fetched for the current registration
Pre-conditions: Rainbow returns owners for the given registrationKey
Test Steps: 1. Trigger build subflow for registrationKey 818 | 2. Capture outbound request | 3. Inspect owners
Expected Result: Rainbow receives GET /owners filtered by toDate eq null and registrationKey eq 818 and active owners are returned

Test Name: Registration with zero owners is skipped
Description: Verifies registrations that return no owners are filtered out
Pre-conditions: Rainbow returns an empty owners list for a registration
Test Steps: 1. Trigger build subflow | 2. Inspect result and logs
Expected Result: The subflow returns an empty list logs Skipping registrationKey=999 no owners and the registration is excluded from upsert

Test Name: Enrich owner with business partner details
Description: Verifies each owner is enriched via the businessPartners endpoint
Pre-conditions: Rainbow Operation OData reachable
Test Steps: 1. Trigger build subflow with one owner | 2. Capture outbound request | 3. Inspect built record
Expected Result: Rainbow Operation receives GET /businessPartners?$filter=id eq 11 and name idtype address and legal id are populated

Description: Verifies registration owner and partner are combined into a flat LandOwner__c
Pre-conditions: Valid registration owner and business partner data available
Test Steps: 1. Trigger build subflow | 2. Inspect produced record
Expected Result: A flat record is produced with External_Key__c=55021 Name Idtype__c IdNumber__c Address__c Essence__c and OwnershipPercent__c

Description: Verifies the Name field is capped at 80 characters
Pre-conditions: Business partner name longer than 80 characters
Test Steps: 1. Provide a 100-char name | 2. Trigger build | 3. Inspect Name length
Expected Result: Name is truncated to the first 80 characters

Description: Verifies records are built when legal id number is absent
Pre-conditions: Owner and partner have no legal id number
Test Steps: 1. Provide owner without legal id | 2. Trigger build | 3. Inspect record
Expected Result: The record is still built with IdNumber__c empty and no error is raised

Test Name: Conditional Building lookup when building exists
Description: Verifies Building__r relationship is set only when buildingCode is present
Pre-conditions: Registration includes a buildingCode
Test Steps: 1. Build record with buildingCode 7976 | 2. Inspect record
Expected Result: Record contains Building__r with BuildingCode__c=7976 linking it to building and project

Test Name: No Building lookup when building absent
Description: Verifies the building reference is omitted when buildingCode is null
Pre-conditions: Registration has no buildingCode
Test Steps: 1. Build record with null buildingCode | 2. Inspect record
Expected Result: Record omits BuildingCode__c and Building__r and is linked at project level only

Description: Verifies Project__c is written as an external-id relationship
Pre-conditions: Registration includes a project_code
Test Steps: 1. Build record with project_code PRJ-1024 | 2. Inspect record
Expected Result: Record contains Project__r with Project_Code__c=PRJ-1024 and Project__c is not written directly

Test Name: Upsert new records by external key
Description: Verifies new LandOwner__c records are created via upsert
Pre-conditions: Salesforce reachable; records do not yet exist
Test Steps: 1. Run with new records | 2. Query Salesforce by External_Key__c
Expected Result: Salesforce upsert on LandOwner__c using External_Key__c creates the records and upsertCount reflects them

Description: Verifies a second run with the same data updates existing records
Pre-conditions: A prior run created the records
Test Steps: 1. Run sync twice with identical data | 2. Count records by External_Key__c
Expected Result: The second run updates the existing record and no duplicate is created

Description: Verifies records are upserted in chunks of the configured batch size
Pre-conditions: More records than one batch
Test Steps: 1. Provide 450 records with batchSize 200 | 2. Trigger upsert | 3. Inspect upsert calls
Expected Result: Records are chunked into batches of 200 (200/200/50) and all are upserted

Description: Verifies a Rainbow connectivity error retries and the run continues with next registration
Pre-conditions: Rainbow returns connection errors for one registration
Test Steps: 1. Force CONNECTIVITY on registrationKey 818 | 2. Trigger run | 3. Inspect logs
Expected Result: The call retries up to 3 times then logs WARN Rainbow fetch failed registrationKey=818 and processing continues with the next registration

Description: Verifies a Rainbow timeout is retried and does not abort the run
Pre-conditions: Rainbow delays beyond rainbow.timeout.ms
Test Steps: 1. Force timeout on one registration | 2. Trigger run | 3. Inspect logs
Expected Result: The timeout is retried then logged as WARN and the overall run completes for remaining registrations

Description: Verifies a Salesforce connectivity error propagates and fails the run
Pre-conditions: Salesforce unreachable
Test Steps: 1. Make Salesforce unreachable | 2. Trigger run | 3. Inspect logs
Expected Result: The error is propagated by the global handler the run is marked failed and logged at ERROR with correlationId

Test Name: Per-record upsert failure logged and others committed
Description: Verifies an item-level upsert failure is logged while valid records still commit
Pre-conditions: One record violates a Salesforce field rule
Test Steps: 1. Include one invalid record among valid ones | 2. Trigger upsert | 3. Inspect logs and Salesforce
Expected Result: The invalid record is logged WARN Upsert failed externalKey=BAD1 and the 5 valid records are committed

Test Name: Uncaught error logged by default handler
Description: Verifies any uncaught error is logged at ERROR with correlationId
Pre-conditions: A processor throws an unexpected error
Test Steps: 1. Inject an unexpected error | 2. Trigger run | 3. Inspect logs
Expected Result: The global default error handler logs the full error and correlationId at ERROR level

Description: Verifies the local health listener responds
Pre-conditions: App running on the configured http.port
Test Steps: 1. Send GET http://localhost:8081/health | 2. Inspect response
Expected Result: The endpoint returns HTTP 200 with body OK

Description: Verifies secure credentials are read from secure config and not hardcoded
Pre-conditions: secure-config.yaml configured with valid encrypted values
Test Steps: 1. Start app with secure properties key | 2. Inspect startup
Expected Result: The app starts resolving secure placeholders and no secret is logged in plain text

Description: Verifies a clear failure when CHANGE_ME placeholders are left unset
Pre-conditions: config left with CHANGE_ME values
Test Steps: 1. Start app with CHANGE_ME credentials | 2. Attempt a run | 3. Inspect error
Expected Result: Authentication to Rainbow or Salesforce fails and the error is logged clearly

Description: Verifies the closing log line reports upserted deleted and skipped counts
Pre-conditions: A run with a mix of new expired and empty-owner registrations
Test Steps: 1. Trigger a mixed run | 2. Inspect the END log line
Expected Result: END landowner-sync reports upserted deleted and skipped counts matching the processed data
